# Ray.Di -- Comprehensive LLM Reference

Ray.Di is a PHP dependency injection framework inspired by Google Guice. It resolves dependencies at compile time via code generation, supports AOP method interception, and requires PHP 7.2+. Install with `composer require ray/di ^2.0`.

Key imports used throughout:

```php
use Ray\Di\AbstractModule;
use Ray\Di\Injector;
use Ray\Di\Di\Inject;
use Ray\Di\Di\Named;
use Ray\Di\Di\Qualifier;
use Ray\Di\Di\PostConstruct;
use Ray\Di\Di\Assisted;
use Ray\Di\ProviderInterface;
use Ray\Di\Scope;
use Ray\Di\InjectionPoints;
use Ray\Di\Multibinder;
use Ray\Di\Map;
use Ray\Di\Di\Set;
use Ray\Aop\MethodInterceptor;
use Ray\Aop\MethodInvocation;
```

## Modules

All bindings are configured in modules. Extend `AbstractModule` and override `configure()`.

```php
class AppModule extends AbstractModule
{
    protected function configure(): void
    {
        // Bindings go here
        $this->bind(LoggerInterface::class)->to(FileLogger::class);
    }
}
```

### Creating an Injector

```php
$injector = new Injector(new AppModule());
$logger = $injector->getInstance(LoggerInterface::class);
```

Pass multiple modules as an array:

```php
$injector = new Injector([new DbModule(), new CacheModule()]);
```

### install() -- Composing Modules

```php
class AppModule extends AbstractModule
{
    protected function configure(): void
    {
        $this->install(new DatabaseModule());
        $this->install(new AuthModule());
        $this->install(new CacheModule());
    }
}
```

Earlier bindings take priority. If `DatabaseModule` and `AuthModule` both bind the same interface, the one installed first wins.

### override() -- Replacing Bindings

```php
$module = new AppModule();
$module->override(new TestModule());
$injector = new Injector($module);
```

Bindings in `TestModule` replace matching bindings in `AppModule`. Use this for testing or environment-specific configuration.

You can also call `override()` inside `configure()`:

```php
protected function configure(): void
{
    $this->install(new ProductionModule());
    $this->override(new StagingOverrides());
}
```

## Linked Bindings

Map an interface to an implementation class. This is the most common binding type.

```php
$this->bind(TransactionLogInterface::class)->to(DatabaseTransactionLog::class);
$this->bind(CreditCardProcessorInterface::class)->to(PaypalProcessor::class);
```

Ray.Di recursively resolves all constructor dependencies of the target class.

## Instance Bindings

Bind a type to a specific instance. Use for value objects and simple values.

```php
$this->bind(UserInterface::class)->toInstance(new User());
$this->bind()->annotatedWith('login_id')->toInstance('bear');
$this->bind()->annotatedWith('max_retries')->toInstance(3);
```

Avoid `toInstance()` with complex objects that are expensive to create -- it runs during module initialization.

## Provider Bindings

For complex object construction logic, bind to a provider class.

```php
$this->bind(TransactionLogInterface::class)->toProvider(DatabaseTransactionLogProvider::class);
```

Provider class:

```php
class DatabaseTransactionLogProvider implements ProviderInterface
{
    public function __construct(
        private readonly ConnectionInterface $connection
    ) {}

    public function get(): DatabaseTransactionLog
    {
        $transactionLog = new DatabaseTransactionLog();
        $transactionLog->setConnection($this->connection);

        return $transactionLog;
    }
}
```

Providers can have their own injected dependencies (like `$connection` above).

### InjectionPoint in Providers

Providers can inspect where they are being injected via `InjectionPointInterface`:

```php
class Psr3LoggerProvider implements ProviderInterface
{
    public function __construct(
        private readonly InjectionPointInterface $ip
    ) {}

    public function get(): \Monolog\Logger
    {
        $logger = new \Monolog\Logger($this->ip->getClass()->getName());
        $logger->pushHandler(new StreamHandler('path/to/your.log', Logger::WARNING));

        return $logger;
    }
}
```

Available methods on `InjectionPointInterface`:

```php
$ip->getClass();      // \ReflectionClass
$ip->getMethod();     // \ReflectionMethod
$ip->getParameter();  // \ReflectionParameter
$ip->getQualifiers(); // array of qualifier attributes
```

## Constructor Bindings

For third-party classes where you cannot add attributes, use `toConstructor()`.

```php
$this->bind($interfaceName)->toConstructor(
    $className,       // string: Class name
    $name,            // array|string: Parameter-to-qualifier mapping
    $injectionPoints, // InjectionPoints: Setter injection config
    $postConstruct    // string: Method name to call after injection
);
```

### PDO Example

```php
$this->bind(\PDO::class)->toConstructor(
    \PDO::class,
    [
        'dsn' => 'pdo_dsn',
        'username' => 'pdo_username',
        'password' => 'pdo_password',
    ]
)->in(Scope::SINGLETON);

$this->bind()->annotatedWith('pdo_dsn')->toInstance('mysql:host=localhost;dbname=mydb');
$this->bind()->annotatedWith('pdo_username')->toInstance(getenv('DB_USER'));
$this->bind()->annotatedWith('pdo_password')->toInstance(getenv('DB_PASSWORD'));
```

### With Setter Injection and PostConstruct

```php
$this->bind(ClientInterface::class)->toConstructor(
    GuzzleClient::class,
    'base_uri=api_endpoint',
    (new InjectionPoints())
        ->addMethod('setLogger')
        ->addOptionalMethod('setCache'),
    'initialize'
);
```

The `$name` parameter accepts a query-string format: `'param1=qualifier1&param2=qualifier2'`

## Untargeted Bindings

Register a concrete class without a target. Useful for preparing dependencies eagerly or applying scope.

```php
$this->bind(MyConcreteClass::class);
$this->bind(AnotherConcreteClass::class)->in(Scope::SINGLETON);
```

## Null Object Binding

Generate and bind a null implementation of an interface. Methods return null/default values.

```php
$this->bind(CreditCardProcessorInterface::class)->toNull();
```

## Scopes

By default, Ray.Di creates a new instance each time. Use `Scope::SINGLETON` for shared instances.

```php
$this->bind(TransactionLogInterface::class)->to(InMemoryTransactionLog::class)->in(Scope::SINGLETON);
```

Singleton scope can be combined with any binding type:

```php
$this->bind(CacheInterface::class)->toProvider(RedisCacheProvider::class)->in(Scope::SINGLETON);
$this->bind(\PDO::class)->toConstructor(\PDO::class, '...')->in(Scope::SINGLETON);
```

## Injection Patterns

### Constructor Injection (default)

No attribute required. Ray.Di automatically injects all constructor parameters.

```php
class BillingService
{
    public function __construct(
        private readonly CreditCardProcessorInterface $processor,
        private readonly TransactionLogInterface $transactionLog
    ) {}
}
```

### Setter Injection

Use `#[Inject]` on setter methods:

```php
class BillingService
{
    private DbInterface $db;

    #[Inject]
    public function setDb(DbInterface $db): void
    {
        $this->db = $db;
    }
}
```

### Optional Injection

Use `#[Inject(optional: true)]` for dependencies that may not be bound:

```php
class PayPalProcessor implements CreditCardProcessorInterface
{
    private const SANDBOX_API_KEY = 'development-use-only';
    private string $apiKey = self::SANDBOX_API_KEY;

    #[Inject(optional: true)]
    public function setApiKey(#[Named('paypal-apikey')] string $apiKey): void
    {
        $this->apiKey = $apiKey;
    }
}
```

### Assisted Injection

Inject dependencies into arbitrary method calls at runtime. Add `#[Assisted]` to parameters with `null` defaults:

```php
class OrderService
{
    public function processOrder(string $orderId, #[Assisted] DbInterface $db = null): void
    {
        // $db is injected by Ray.Di at call time
    }
}
```

### Property Injection

Ray.Di does NOT support property injection. Use constructor or setter injection.

## Qualifiers -- Distinguishing Same-Type Bindings

### #[Named] -- Built-in String Qualifier

```php
// In the consumer class:
public function __construct(
    #[Named('checkout')] private readonly CreditCardProcessorInterface $processor,
    #[Named('backup')] private readonly CreditCardProcessorInterface $backup
) {}

// In the module:
$this->bind(CreditCardProcessorInterface::class)
    ->annotatedWith('checkout')
    ->to(CheckoutProcessor::class);
$this->bind(CreditCardProcessorInterface::class)
    ->annotatedWith('backup')
    ->to(BackupProcessor::class);
```

### Custom Qualifier Attributes (Type-Safe)

Define a qualifier attribute:

```php
use Ray\Di\Di\Qualifier;

#[Attribute, Qualifier]
final class PayPal
{
}
```

Use it on constructor parameters:

```php
public function __construct(
    #[PayPal] private readonly CreditCardProcessorInterface $processor
) {}
```

Bind it in the module:

```php
$this->bind(CreditCardProcessorInterface::class)
    ->annotatedWith(PayPal::class)
    ->to(PayPalProcessor::class);
```

### Qualifier with Parameter Name

When a qualifier should only apply to a specific parameter:

```php
public function __construct(
    #[PayPal('processor')] private readonly CreditCardProcessorInterface $processor,
    private readonly OtherInterface $other
) {}
```

### Qualifier with Enum Values

Reuse a single qualifier attribute with different values:

```php
enum PaymentType
{
    case CREDIT;
    case DEBIT;
    case CRYPTO;
}

#[Attribute, Qualifier]
final class Payment
{
    public function __construct(
        public readonly PaymentType $value
    ) {}
}
```

Usage: `#[Payment(PaymentType::CREDIT)]`, `#[Payment(PaymentType::DEBIT)]`, etc.

### Binding Scalar/Untyped Values with Qualifiers

When a parameter has no type hint, use `annotatedWith` with an empty `bind()`:

```php
// Module:
$this->bind()->annotatedWith(Message::class)->toInstance('Hello %s!');
$this->bind()->annotatedWith('api_key')->toInstance('sk-12345');

// Consumer:
public function __construct(
    #[Message] private readonly string $message,
    #[Named('api_key')] private readonly string $apiKey
) {}
```

## Object Lifecycle -- #[PostConstruct]

A method annotated with `#[PostConstruct]` is called after all dependencies are injected:

```php
use Ray\Di\Di\PostConstruct;

class MyService
{
    private DbInterface $db;

    public function __construct(DbInterface $db)
    {
        $this->db = $db;
    }

    #[PostConstruct]
    public function init(): void
    {
        // Called after constructor and setter injection complete
        $this->db->connect();
    }
}
```

## AOP -- Method Interception

Ray.Di supports aspect-oriented programming via method interceptors for cross-cutting concerns (logging, transactions, security, caching).

### Step 1: Define an Attribute

```php
#[Attribute(Attribute::TARGET_METHOD)]
final class Cacheable
{
}
```

### Step 2: Apply to Methods

```php
class UserRepository implements UserRepositoryInterface
{
    #[Cacheable]
    public function findById(int $id): User
    {
        // expensive database query
    }
}
```

### Step 3: Implement the Interceptor

```php
use Ray\Aop\MethodInterceptor;
use Ray\Aop\MethodInvocation;

class CacheInterceptor implements MethodInterceptor
{
    public function __construct(
        private readonly CacheInterface $cache
    ) {}

    public function invoke(MethodInvocation $invocation): mixed
    {
        $key = $invocation->getMethod()->getName() . serialize($invocation->getArguments()->getArrayCopy());
        $cached = $this->cache->get($key);
        if ($cached !== null) {
            return $cached;
        }

        $result = $invocation->proceed();
        $this->cache->set($key, $result);

        return $result;
    }
}
```

### Step 4: Bind the Interceptor in a Module

```php
class CacheModule extends AbstractModule
{
    protected function configure(): void
    {
        $this->bind(CacheInterface::class)->to(RedisCache::class)->in(Scope::SINGLETON);
        $this->bindInterceptor(
            $this->matcher->any(),                          // match any class
            $this->matcher->annotatedWith('Cacheable'),     // match #[Cacheable] methods
            [CacheInterceptor::class]                       // interceptor classes
        );
    }
}
```

### Matcher Methods

- `$this->matcher->any()` -- matches all classes/methods
- `$this->matcher->annotatedWith('AttributeName')` -- matches classes/methods with the given attribute

### MethodInvocation API

Inside an interceptor:

```php
$invocation->proceed();              // Call the original method
$invocation->getMethod();            // \ReflectionMethod
$invocation->getArguments();         // \ArrayObject of arguments
$invocation->getThis();              // The target object instance
$invocation->getMethod()->getName(); // Method name
```

### Disabling an Interceptor

```php
use Ray\Aop\NullInterceptor;

$this->bind(SomeInterceptor::class)->to(NullInterceptor::class);
```

### AOP Limitations

- Classes must be non-final
- Methods must be public and non-final
- Instances must be created by Ray.Di (not `new`)

## Injecting Providers -- Lazy/Multiple Instances

When you need lazy loading, multiple instances, or to mix scopes, inject `ProviderInterface` instead of the type directly.

```php
use Ray\Di\Di\Set;

class BillingService
{
    /**
     * @param ProviderInterface<CreditCardProcessorInterface> $processorProvider
     */
    public function __construct(
        #[Set(CreditCardProcessorInterface::class)] private readonly ProviderInterface $processorProvider
    ) {}

    public function charge(): void
    {
        // A new or cached instance depending on scope
        $processor = $this->processorProvider->get();
    }
}
```

Use `#[Set(InterfaceName::class)]` to specify which type the provider produces. Add `@param ProviderInterface<T>` phpdoc for static analysis.

### Use Cases

Lazy loading -- defer expensive construction:

```php
public function logFailure(ChargeResult $result): void
{
    if (!$result->wasSuccessful()) {
        $connection = $this->connectionProvider->get(); // only created when needed
    }
}
```

Multiple instances -- get a fresh instance each call:

```php
$entry1 = $this->entryProvider->get();
$entry2 = $this->entryProvider->get(); // different instance (unless singleton)
```

Mixing scopes -- singleton depends on request-scoped data:

```php
class SingletonService
{
    public function __construct(
        #[Set(User::class)] private readonly ProviderInterface $userProvider
    ) {}

    public function handle(): void
    {
        $user = $this->userProvider->get(); // fresh per request
    }
}
```

## Contextual Provider Bindings

Same interface, different implementations based on context. Pass a context string as the second argument to `toProvider()`.

```php
// Module:
$dbConfig = ['user' => $userDsn, 'job' => $jobDsn, 'log' => $logDsn];
$this->bind()->annotatedWith('db_config')->toInstance($dbConfig);
$this->bind(Connection::class)->annotatedWith('usr_db')->toProvider(DbalProvider::class, 'user');
$this->bind(Connection::class)->annotatedWith('job_db')->toProvider(DbalProvider::class, 'job');
$this->bind(Connection::class)->annotatedWith('log_db')->toProvider(DbalProvider::class, 'log');
```

Provider implementing `SetContextInterface`:

```php
use Ray\Di\SetContextInterface;

class DbalProvider implements ProviderInterface, SetContextInterface
{
    private string $context;

    public function __construct(
        #[Named('db_config')] private readonly array $dbConfigs
    ) {}

    public function setContext(string $context): void
    {
        $this->context = $context;
    }

    public function get(): Connection
    {
        return DriverManager::getConnection($this->dbConfigs[$this->context]);
    }
}
```

Consumer:

```php
public function __construct(
    #[Named('usr_db')] private readonly Connection $userDb,
    #[Named('job_db')] private readonly Connection $jobDb,
    #[Named('log_db')] private readonly Connection $logDb
) {}
```

## Multibindings

Bind multiple implementations of the same interface. Useful for plugin architectures.

### Multibinder (Set)

Register implementations in modules:

```php
class PluginModule extends AbstractModule
{
    protected function configure(): void
    {
        $uriBinder = Multibinder::newInstance($this, UriSummarizerInterface::class);
        $uriBinder->addBinding()->to(FlickrSummarizer::class);
        $uriBinder->addBinding()->to(GoogleMapsSummarizer::class);
    }
}
```

Inject the set using `#[Set]` and `Map`:

```php
class TweetPrettifier
{
    /**
     * @param Map<UriSummarizerInterface> $summarizers
     */
    public function __construct(
        #[Set(UriSummarizerInterface::class)] private readonly Map $summarizers
    ) {}

    public function prettifyUri(Uri $uri): string
    {
        foreach ($this->summarizers as $summarizer) {
            $summary = $summarizer->summarize($uri);
            if ($summary !== null) {
                return $summary;
            }
        }

        return $uri->toString();
    }
}
```

### MapBinder (Named Set)

Add named entries for key-based lookup:

```php
$uriBinder = Multibinder::newInstance($this, UriSummarizerInterface::class);
$uriBinder->addBinding('flickr')->to(FlickrSummarizer::class);
$uriBinder->addBinding('youtube')->to(YouTubeSummarizer::class);
```

Access by key:

```php
$flickr = $this->summarizers['flickr'];
```

### setBinding() -- Override Previous Binding

```php
$uriBinder->setBinding('flickr')->to(NewFlickrSummarizer::class);
```

### Composing Plugin Modules

```php
class AppModule extends AbstractModule
{
    protected function configure(): void
    {
        $this->install(new CoreModule());
        $this->install(new FlickrPluginModule());
        $this->install(new YouTubePluginModule());
    }
}
```

Multiple modules can contribute to the same `Multibinder` -- `Multibinder::newInstance()` does not override existing bindings.

## Performance -- Production Optimization

### ScriptInjector (Recommended for Production)

Compiles bindings to raw PHP factory code. Fastest option.

```php
use Ray\Di\ScriptInjector;
use Ray\Compiler\DiCompiler;
use Ray\Compiler\Exception\NotCompiled;

try {
    $injector = new ScriptInjector($tmpDir);
    $instance = $injector->getInstance(ListerInterface::class);
} catch (NotCompiled $e) {
    $compiler = new DiCompiler(new AppModule(), $tmpDir);
    $compiler->compile();
    $instance = $injector->getInstance(ListerInterface::class);
}
```

### Serialized Injector (Cache)

```php
// Compile once:
$injector = new Injector(new AppModule());
$cached = serialize($injector);
file_put_contents('injector.cache', $cached);

// Load from cache:
$injector = unserialize(file_get_contents('injector.cache'));
$service = $injector->getInstance(ServiceInterface::class);
```

### PHP 8 Attribute Reader (Development Performance)

In `composer.json`:

```json
{
    "autoload": {
        "files": [
            "vendor/ray/aop/attribute_reader.php"
        ]
    }
}
```

Or in bootstrap:

```php
use Koriym\Attributes\AttributeReader;
use Ray\ServiceLocator\ServiceLocator;

ServiceLocator::setReader(new AttributeReader());
```

## Dependency Graph Visualization

Generate a GraphViz `.dot` file for your object graph:

```php
use Ray\ObjectGrapher\ObjectGrapher;

$dot = (new ObjectGrapher())(new AppModule());
file_put_contents('graph.dot', $dot);
```

Render with `dot -T png graph.dot > graph.png` or paste into [GraphvizOnline](https://dreampuf.github.io/GraphvizOnline/).

## Complete Example -- Putting It All Together

```php
<?php
require __DIR__ . '/vendor/autoload.php';

use Ray\Di\AbstractModule;
use Ray\Di\Di\Qualifier;
use Ray\Di\Injector;
use Ray\Di\ProviderInterface;

// 1. Define qualifier attributes
#[Attribute, Qualifier]
class Message {}

#[Attribute, Qualifier]
class Count {}

// 2. Define providers for scalar values
class MessageProvider implements ProviderInterface
{
    public function get(): string
    {
        return 'hello world';
    }
}

class CountProvider implements ProviderInterface
{
    public function get(): int
    {
        return 3;
    }
}

// 3. Define the module
class DemoModule extends AbstractModule
{
    protected function configure(): void
    {
        $this->bind()->annotatedWith(Message::class)->toProvider(MessageProvider::class);
        $this->bind()->annotatedWith(Count::class)->toProvider(CountProvider::class);
    }
}

// 4. Define the class with injected dependencies
class Greeter
{
    public function __construct(
        #[Message] private readonly string $message,
        #[Count] private readonly int $count
    ) {}

    public function sayHello(): void
    {
        for ($i = 0; $i < $this->count; $i++) {
            echo $this->message . PHP_EOL;
        }
    }
}

// 5. Bootstrap
$injector = new Injector(new DemoModule());
$greeter = $injector->getInstance(Greeter::class);
$greeter->sayHello();
```

## Best Practices Summary

- Use constructor injection with `readonly` properties for immutable objects
- Do not inject the Injector itself -- declare dependencies statically
- Inject only direct dependencies -- use providers to extract indirect ones
- Organize modules by feature, not by class type
- Modules should be fast and side-effect free -- no DB connections in `configure()`
- Avoid conditional logic in modules -- use separate modules for different environments
- Do not reuse qualifier attributes for unrelated bindings
- Avoid static state -- inject everything
- Avoid cyclic dependencies -- extract shared state into a new class, or use providers
- Use `Injector` as few times as possible (ideally once at bootstrap)

## Framework Integrations

- [BEAR.Sunday](http://bearsunday.github.io/)
- [Laravel](https://github.com/ray-di/Ray.RayDiForLaravel)
- [CakePHP PipingBag](https://github.com/lorenzo/piping-bag)
- [Yii 1](https://github.com/koriym/Ray.Dyii)

## Binding DSL Quick Reference

| DSL | Effect |
|-----|--------|
| `bind(I::class)->to(C::class)` | Linked binding: I resolves to C |
| `bind(I::class)->toProvider(P::class)` | Provider binding: I created by P |
| `bind(I::class)->toInstance($obj)` | Instance binding: I returns $obj |
| `bind(I::class)->toConstructor(C::class, [...])` | Constructor binding for third-party classes |
| `bind(I::class)->toNull()` | Null object binding |
| `bind(C::class)` | Untargeted binding (concrete class) |
| `bind(I::class)->to(C::class)->in(Scope::SINGLETON)` | Singleton scope |
| `bind(I::class)->annotatedWith(Q::class)->to(C::class)` | Qualified with custom attribute |
| `bind(I::class)->annotatedWith('name')->to(C::class)` | Qualified with #[Named('name')] |
| `bind()->annotatedWith('key')->toInstance($val)` | Bind scalar/untyped value by name |
| `bind()->annotatedWith(Q::class)->toInstance($val)` | Bind scalar/untyped value by qualifier |
| `install(new M())` | Install another module |
| `override(new M())` | Override bindings with another module |
| `bindInterceptor($classMatcher, $methodMatcher, [...])` | Bind AOP interceptor |
